intake.user_request: Promote STUB-RAG-INDEX-SEAM-DELETION-001 (CORE bbdeba5, DISPATCH-043) into a fully-scoped WO. Bulletproof concept #3 of 6: walk src/integration/rag/* end-to-end and delete seams that don't earn keep — silent skips, try/catch swallowers, dead-code branches, pass-through layers. CORE-flagged anchor case: chunk-converter.ts:60-63 silent fileExists skip with three resolution options (A/B/C) requiring ORCHESTRATOR ruling.
intake.predecessor_safety_net_wo: WO-RAG-INDEX-FROZEN-FIXTURE-DUAL-AC-INVARIANTS-001 (archived 2026-05-06, CORE 282382a) — CI-blocking dual-AC invariant in place

classification.plan_type_rationale: Phase 1 = seam inventory (walk src/integration/rag/* and src/integration/rag/__tests__/*; enumerate every try/catch swallower, silent skip, dead-code branch, pass-through layer; per-seam disposition = delete | promote-to-explicit-failure | keep-with-justification). Phase 1 also surfaces the chunk-converter.ts:60-63 anchor case A/B/C decision as a scope_decision_required block — ORCHESTRATOR rules before Phase 2. Phase 2+ = atomic-commit removals (one seam per commit) gated by tsc + frozen-fixture invariant + AC-05a/b real-world identity preservation. Investigation phase is non-skippable: the seam list is unknown at scoping time and the per-seam disposition is the load-bearing decision per seam.
classification.plan_type_decided_by: SKILLS scoping (DISPATCH-047 dispatch payload: 'Plan_type likely phased_implementation (seam inventory before atomic removal)'). SKILLS confirms phased; investigation is required, not optional.
classification.discovery: Stub markdown enumerates 4 broad inventory targets (chunk-converter.ts, indexing-orchestrator.ts, rag/* broader sweep, rag/__tests__/*). Anchor case (chunk-converter.ts:60-63) verified at line numbers. Earning-keep criteria + dead-weight signals enumerated in stub.

in_scope: Phase 1: walk src/integration/rag/* (production) + src/integration/rag/__tests__/* (tests) and produce a seam inventory document with per-seam disposition (delete | promote-to-explicit-failure | keep-with-justification + comment)
Phase 1: surface chunk-converter.ts:60-63 anchor case A/B/C ruling-required block to ORCHESTRATOR before Phase 2 starts
Phase 2+: atomic-commit removals (one seam per commit; ~5-15 commits anticipated based on stub inventory targets)
Per-seam removals must EITHER delete cleanly OR promote to explicit-failure semantics (throw, log, count in IndexingResult) — silent fallback is the anti-pattern being eliminated
Per-commit gates: tsc clean both configs, frozen-fixture CI-blocking invariant still PASSES, AC-05a real-world 2415===2415 + AC-05b real-world 263===263 still PASS
Tests that currently exercise the silent-skip branches are updated to expect explicit failures (or deleted if branch was dead)
Seam inventory doc + per-seam disposition rationale lands in WO directory as inventory.md (or analogous artifact) for archive evidence
out_of_scope: Phase 7 archive — locked, MUST NOT be reopened

Predecessor archives (rag-index-frozen-fixture-dual-ac-invariants, rag-index-single-analyzer-slice, cleanup-indexing-orchestrator-analyzerservice-param, path-normalization-fix, pipeline-indexing-rag) — all CLOSED, must NOT be modified
AnalyzerService source code (src/analyzer/analyzer-service.ts) — still used by populate-coderef + BreakingChangeDetector
Production code outside src/integration/rag/* — boundary violation
Frozen-fixture file contents (src/integration/rag/__tests__/fixtures/dual-ac-frozen/) — frozen by definition; if a seam removal requires fixture changes, HALT and reframe
The dual-AC fixture and frozen-fixture invariant ARE the substrate-identity safety net — exempt from seam audit per stub directive
Schema reduction (concept #5), branded paths (concept #4), two-grain drop (concept #6) — separate WOs
Any new IndexingResult schema fields BEYOND the anchor-case Option A path (which itself requires ORCHESTRATOR ruling on DR-PHASE-7-B locked-additive constraint)

scope_decision_required.decision: chunk-converter.ts:60-63 silent fileExists skip — choose Option A, B, or C
scope_decision_required.ruling_required_before: Phase 2 atomic removals begin (Phase 1 inventory may run before ruling, but the anchor case removal commit cannot land until ORCHESTRATOR rules)

option_a_earn_keep.summary: Convert silent skip to chunksSkipped with new SkipReason='phantom_import_target'; surface count in IndexingResult; add fixture assertion that no real-codebase scan produces unexpected phantoms
option_a_earn_keep.pro: Earns keep — the seam handles a real failure mode (phantom imports) with explicit observable semantics. Most defensible if phantom imports actually occur on real coderef-core scans.
option_a_earn_keep.con: Requires new SkipReason enum value; touches DR-PHASE-7-B locked-additive constraint on IndexingResult. ORCHESTRATOR must rule on whether DR-PHASE-7-B allows the addition.
option_a_earn_keep.risk: Low — additive only. Frozen-fixture invariant tests are not affected (their input has no phantoms).
option_a_earn_keep.blast_radius: src/integration/rag/chunk-converter.ts (~5-10 LOC for SkipReason+counter), src/integration/rag/types.ts or wherever SkipReason enum lives (1 line), tests covering chunksSkippedDetails (small)

option_b_delete.summary: Remove the fileExists check entirely; let the read-error path (lines 65-77) handle missing files via existing errors[] accumulator
option_b_delete.pro: Most aggressive cleanup. Eliminates silent skip without adding schema surface. Honors DR-PHASE-7-B without exception.
option_b_delete.con: If phantom imports DO occur on real scans, they get surfaced as readFile errors instead of skipped silently — a behavior change. Errors[] accumulator may not track them at the same granularity as the current skip path.
option_b_delete.risk: Medium — behavior change for any phantom-import case. Frozen-fixture invariant unaffected (no phantoms in input). AC-05a/b real-world identity preservation requires verification: confirm that on real coderef-core scan, no files hit lines 60-63 today (or only files that the read-error path handles equivalently).
option_b_delete.blast_radius: src/integration/rag/chunk-converter.ts (delete ~5 LOC), tests that mock the fileExists branch (delete or update)

option_c_keep_but_log.summary: Add a console.warn or logger call inside the silent-skip branch so the drop becomes observable in CI output (even if not surfaced in IndexingResult)
option_c_keep_but_log.pro: Minimal change. Restores observability without schema surface. No behavior change for the chunk pipeline.
option_c_keep_but_log.con: Half-measure — warnings don't fail CI, callers can't assert on them, and logs are noisy at scale. Doesn't earn keep on the criteria-1 test ("surfaces a real failure mode explicitly via assertable mechanism"). Likely to be re-flagged in a future seam audit.
option_c_keep_but_log.risk: Low — purely additive log line. Honors all locked constraints. But weakens the bulletproof-#3 substrate by accepting a silent-but-logged seam as the resolved state.

scope_decision_required.skills_recommendation: SKILLS does not pre-rule. Surface per dispatch-047 directive ('If anchor case (chunk-converter.ts:60-63) ambiguous, surface for ORCHESTRATOR ruling'). All three options have legitimate trade-offs; the choice depends on (a) whether DR-PHASE-7-B is hard or negotiable, (b) whether phantom imports actually occur on real coderef-core scans, (c) the degree to which 'earn keep via observable mechanism' is treated as non-negotiable for bulletproof #3.

constraints: Phase 7 archive MUST NOT be reopened
AC-05a real-world 2415===2415 MUST stay PASS (verified post-each-commit via predecessor WO static probe)
Frozen-fixture CI-blocking invariant MUST stay PASS — if any seam removal trips it, HALT and reframe
Frozen-fixture file contents under src/integration/rag/__tests__/fixtures/dual-ac-frozen/ are frozen — do NOT modify; if a removal requires fixture change, HALT
Each seam removal must EITHER delete cleanly OR promote to explicit-failure semantics — do NOT replace one silent seam with another
tsc --noEmit clean both configs after each atomic commit
Cultural validator scope-mode + tracking validator scope-mode PASS clean (acknowledge pre-existing 14/15 stubs.json schema drift per STUB-044)
Net negative LOC required (real cleanup, not zero-sum) — plan should target measurable reduction
Anchor case ruling REQUIRED before Phase 2 atomic-removal commit for that specific seam lands

anchor_case_verified.code: const fileExists = await this.fileExists(filePath); if (!fileExists) { continue; }
anchor_case_verified.issue: Silent skip with zero observable signal — no counter, no log, no entry in chunksSkippedDetails, no failure surface

stub_inventory_targets_at_scoping: src/integration/rag/chunk-converter.ts (silent skips, fallback branches, type-coercion fallbacks)
src/integration/rag/* broader sweep (embedding adapters, vector-store adapters, pass-through layers, helper wrappers)
src/integration/rag/__tests__/* (silent-mock antipatterns; frozen-fixture + dual-AC tests EXEMPT)
earning_keep_criteria_from_stub: Surfaces a real failure mode explicitly (throw, log-to-CI, IndexingResult counter)
Exercised by a test (happy-path + failure mode it handles)
Carries a single-sentence comment explaining WHY (failure mode), not WHAT (workaround)
dead_weight_signals_from_stub: Swallows errors with no log, no throw, no counter
Pass-through layer with no added semantics
Comment justifies the workaround instead of the failure mode
Added 'for safety' with no documented failure mode
evidence_summary.safety_net_status: WO-RAG-INDEX-FROZEN-FIXTURE-DUAL-AC-INVARIANTS-001 archived 2026-05-06 (CORE 282382a). Frozen-fixture invariant CI-blocking confirmed via mutation-guard Path A. Any seam removal that silently shifts AC-05a/b on synthetic input will trip the invariant immediately.

pending_artifacts.plan_json: Authored at scoping seed (Phase 1 inventory + scope_decision_required surface; Phase 2+ atomic removals contingent on anchor-case ruling)
